steps.title: Recognize the fractional exponent rule
steps.content: A fractional exponent with numerator 1 always represents a root. Specifically, when you see $x^{1/n}$, this means the $n$-th root of $x$.

steps.title: Apply the rule to the given expression
steps.content: We have $x^{1/14}$. Using the rule above with $n = 14$: $$x^{1/14} = \sqrt[14]{x}$$ This is the 14th root of $x$, which matches choice B — B is correct.
